name: Dependent Types
def: Types that can depend on values, letting the type encode rich correctness properties.
problem: Prove program properties (e.g. array bounds, protocol states) statically within the type system.
mechanism: Types are indexed by values (e.g. Vector<n>); the type checker becomes a proof checker verifying constraints at compile time.
benefits: Eliminate whole bug classes by construction
limits: Steep learning curve; proofs are work
Mainstream langs lack them — only encodings
failures: Misusing partial encodings as full guarantees
whenNot: Most application code — cost rarely justified
myths: "Available in mainstream languages" — only weak encodings (const generics, phantom types)
